data.title: National Fuel Gas Co expected to post earnings of $2.80 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• National Fuel Gas Co , is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 29 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Williamsville New York-based company is expected to report a 16.6% increase in revenue to $851.483 million from $729.95 million a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 5 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for National Fuel Gas Co is for earnings of $2.80 per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 3 "strong buy" or "buy," 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for National Fuel Gas Co is $106.00, about 20.2% above its last closing price of $88.18

Previous quarterly performance (using preferred earnings measure in US dollars). ​

data.title: Nasdaq and S&P Advance to Records; Oil Up After Peace Talks Stall — WSJ
data.description:
Oil prices climbed and U.S. stocks wavered after President Trump said he wasn't sending U.S. envoys to Pakistan for peace talks with Iran.

The front-month Brent crude futures contract rose nearly 3% to $108.23 a barrel. Major U.S. stock indexes moved off their lows in afternoon trading, with Nasdaq and the S&P 500 building on their Friday records. The tech-heavy Nasdaq added 0.2% and the S&P 500 moved higher 0.1%, while the Dow industrials faded 0.1%.

In a sign of concern about the consumer, staples stocks lost about 1% with Domino's Pizza's 9% fall turning in the worst performance of the index. Eight of 11 sectors fell, with mega-cap Nvidia's 4% rise carrying the day for benchmarks.

Investors are set to digest a slew of earnings reports this week, with a fifth of S&P 500 companies reporting. Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon and Meta are all set to release first-quarter results Wednesday, with Apple following on Thursday.

It will also be a busy week for central banks, with policy decisions due from the Federal Reserve, the European Central Bank, the Bank of Japan and the Bank of England. The Fed is expected to keep rates unchanged at 3.50-3.75% at its meeting, which wraps up Wednesday.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Qualcomm Inc expected to post earnings of $2.56 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Qualcomm Inc is expected to show a fall in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 29 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The San Diego California-based company is expected to report a 2.2% decrease in revenue to $10.593 billion from $10.84 billion a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 26 analysts, based on LSEG data.

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Qualcomm Inc is for earnings of $2.56 per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 12 "strong buy" or "buy," 25 "hold" and 4 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts had risen by about 0.2% in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Qualcomm Inc is $150.00, about 0.8% above its last closing price of $148.85

Previous quarterly performance (using preferred earnings measure in US dollars). ​
